claims.text: The c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) variant was the most frequent GAA mutation observed in the study cohort, present in 4 out of 10 Chinese patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease.

citations.quote: The most frequent mutation observed in our study was c.1935C > A, with four out of ten patients having this mutation.

claims.text: Patient 0103 (female, Han, age 5.8 months at first infusion, onset age 4 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) in compound heterozygosity with c.2662G>T p.Glu888* (het), was CRIM-positive, had GAA activity 1.05 μmol/L/h (normal 2.88–98.02), LVMI 291.1 g/m², LVEF 61.1%, no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0104 (female, She ethnicity, age 7 months at first infusion, onset age 6 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) in compound heterozygosity with c.1432G>A p.Gly478Arg (het), was CRIM-positive, had GAA activity 0.37 μmol/L/h (normal 2.88–98.02), LVMI 745.5 g/m², LVEF 49.2%, no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0105 (female, Han, age 5.9 months at first infusion, onset age 5 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) in compound heterozygosity with c.1832G>A p.Gly611Asp (het), was CRIM-positive, had GAA activity 1.10 μmol/L/h (normal 2.88–98.02), LVMI 298.8 g/m², LVEF 41.1%, no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0302 (male, Han, age 5.4 months at first infusion, onset age 4 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) in compound heterozygosity with c.2662G>T p.Glu888* (het), was CRIM-positive, had GAA activity 6.8 nmol/g/min (normal 24.8–93.0), LVMI 361.9 g/m², LVEF 79.8%, no ventilator use at baseline.

Patient 0401 (male, Han, age 7.4 months at first infusion, onset age 6 months) carried c.1935C>A p.Asp645Glu (het) in compound heterozygosity with c.1121G>A p.Cys374Tyr (het), was CRIM-positive, had GAA activity 2.4 nmol/h/mgPr (normal 62.3–301.7), LVMI 231.8 g/m², LVEF 44.0%, no ventilator use at baseline.
All four patients carrying c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) were compound heterozygous with a second pathogenic variant and were CRIM-positive, consistent with having at least one missense allele.

citations.quote: If a patient had at least one missense mutation, he or she was considered CRIM-positive.

claims.text: All 10 patients including those carrying c.1935C>A had GAA enzyme activity below the lower limit of the normal reference range, confirming pathogenicity in the context of infantile-onset Pompe disease.

citations.quote: All patients had GAA enzyme test results below the lower limit of the normal reference range.
